name: Cosmological constant smallness
summary: The observed cosmological constant rho_Lambda ~ (2.3 meV)^4 is enormously smaller than any natural QFT vacuum-energy estimate. No symmetry of the Standard Model coupled to gravity protects a small positive Lambda, so its smallness is a 't Hooft-naturalness puzzle of the most extreme kind known.

characteristic_numbers.note: Naive expectation uses a Planck-scale cutoff. Ratio of energy densities is ~10^120, i.e. (gap_log10 in mass)^4.
Cutoff at QCD scale Lambda_QCD ~ 200 MeV. Even this far softer cutoff leaves a ~10^44 hierarchy in energy density.

principles_stressed.note: The smallness of Lambda is the canonical naturalness puzzle: setting Lambda to zero does not restore any known symmetry, so technical naturalness fails.

history: Articulated by Weinberg (1989). Cosmic acceleration discovery in 1998 (Riess, Perlmutter) confirmed Lambda is positive and tiny rather than zero. DESI Y1 (2024) shows a ~2-3 sigma preference for time-evolving dark energy that, if confirmed, deepens rather than resolves the puzzle.

references.citation: Weinberg, 'The cosmological constant problem', Rev. Mod. Phys. 61, 1 (1989)
Martin, 'Everything you always wanted to know about the cosmological constant problem (but were afraid to ask)', CR Physique 13, 566 (2012)
